meaning: To officially end a law, rule, or system.
example: The school wants to abolish homework on weekends.

meaning: To take in liquid, gas, or another substance.
synonyms: take in, soak up, drink in
example: Plants absorb water from the ground.

meaning: Expressing an idea or feeling rather than a real object.
example: "Love" is an abstract word, you cannot touch it.

meaning: Extremely silly, unreasonable, or not logical.
example: It is absurd to go out in the heavy rain without a coat.

meaning: To start moving faster or to make something happen sooner.
example: You need to push the pedal to accelerate the car.

meaning: Easy to find, enter, reach, or talk to.
example: The school library is accessible to all students.

meaning: To provide a place to stay, sit, or sleep for someone.
example: This big hotel can accommodate more than two hundred guests.

meaning: To finish doing something successfully.
example: You can accomplish your goals if you work hard.

meaning: To collect more and more of something over time.
example: Dust can accumulate quickly on these old shelves.

meaning: The state of being correct, exact, or true.
example: The teacher checked the accuracy of my math answers.

meaning: To publicly support a plan, idea, or way of doing things.
example: Many doctors advocate eating fresh vegetables every day.

meaning: A list of things that people need to talk about in a meeting.
example: What is the next item on our meeting agenda?

meaning: To say that someone did something bad, without proof yet.
example: Some people allege that he stole the money.

meaning: A group of countries or people working together to help each other.
example: The two companies formed an alliance to sell new products.

meaning: To give a share of money, time, or space for a special use.
example: The principal will allocate some money to buy new books.

meaning: Having more than one possible meaning, or not clear.
example: His answer was ambiguous, so we did not know what to do.

meaning: To change a law, rule, or written statement slightly to make it better.
example: We need to amend the rules of our club.

meaning: To expect that something will happen and prepare for it.
example: We anticipate that many people will come to the party.

meaning: Supporting or opposing one person or group in an unfair way.
example: The judge showed bias toward his friend during the trial.

meaning: Very strange, unusual, or weird.
example: He wore a bizarre costume to the wedding.

meaning: To make something increase, become better, or stronger.
example: Getting a good grade will boost your confidence.

meaning: To happen at the exact same time as something else.
example: My birthday and the school holiday coincide this year.

meaning: To work together with someone to achieve something.
example: The students will collaborate on a science project.

meaning: To force someone to do something.
example: Heavy rain will compel us to stay at home today.

meaning: To admit that something is true, even if you do not want to.
example: He had to concede that his friend was right.

meaning: To say that what someone else said is wrong or the opposite.
example: The two reports contradict each other, so we don't know the truth.

meaning: To become smaller, fewer, or weaker; or to politely say "no".
example: The price of apples will decline in summer.

meaning: To show someone or something in a picture, painting, or story.
example: The old painting tried to depict a royal family.

meaning: To get something from a specific source.
example: Many English words derive from Latin.

meaning: To completely destroy a place or make someone very sad.
example: A big fire can devastate a beautiful green forest.

meaning: To see or show the difference between two or more things.
example: It is hard to differentiate between the two baby birds.

meaning: To make something become smaller or less important.
example: The medicine helped to diminish the pain in my head.

meaning: To share new or secret information with other people.
example: She refused to disclose the name of her secret friend.

meaning: To change the shape or meaning of something so it is false.
example: Do not distort the truth to make yourself look good.

meaning: Including many different types of people or things.
example: The city has a very diverse population of people.

meaning: Having a lot of details and carefully planned.
example: They made an elaborate plan for the surprise party.

meaning: Graceful, attractive, and beautiful in a simple way.
example: She wore an elegant black dress to the dinner.

meaning: To completely remove or get rid of something.
example: You should eliminate sugary drinks from your meals.

meaning: To hold someone closely in your arms, or to accept a new idea gladly.
example: They love to embrace new technologies in their class.

meaning: Based on real experience or facts, not just ideas or theories.
example: Scientists need empirical proof to show their ideas are right.

meaning: An earnest effort or attempt to do something difficult.
example: Learning a new language is a great endeavor.

meaning: To publicly say that you support or agree with someone or something.
example: Famous athletes often endorse sports shoes on television.

meaning: To improve the quality, amount, or strength of something.
example: You can use fresh spices to enhance the taste of your food.

meaning: Equal in value, size, or meaning to something else.
example: A ten-dollar bill is equivalent to two five-dollar bills.

meaning: To carefully judge the quality, cost, or value of something.
example: The teacher will evaluate our speaking skills today.

meaning: To describe something as larger, better, or worse than it really is.
example: He likes to exaggerate when he tells stories about fishing.

meaning: To use something or someone in an unfair or selfish way.
example: Some bad bosses exploit their workers by paying too little.

meaning: To make an action or a process easier or faster.
example: Clear road signs will facilitate driving in the city.

meaning: To change up and down frequently, rather than staying same.
example: The temperature can fluctuate a lot during spring.

meaning: To help something grow, develop, or improve over time.
example: The school hopes to foster a love for reading books.

meaning: At all times; every time.
example: She always drinks water in the morning.

meaning: To reach a place at the end of a journey.

meaning: Very attractive or pleasing to look at.
example: This is a beautiful flower.

meaning: To start doing something.
example: Let us begin our English class now.

meaning: To pick one thing instead of another.

meaning: Not the same as something else.
example: Dogs and cats are different animals.

meaning: Not difficult; simple to do.

meaning: A person you know well and like.

meaning: Feeling pleased, glad, or satisfied.
example: The children are happy to play outside.

meaning: To get knowledge or skill in something.
example: We learn new words every day.

meaning: To pay attention to a sound.

meaning: Easy to understand or do; not complex.
example: He gave a simple answer.

meaning: With each other or near each other.
example: They walk to school together.

meaning: To know the meaning of something.
example: Do you understand this question?

meaning: To greet someone in a friendly way.
example: We welcome you to our home.

meaning: A group of people who are related to each other.
example: I love spending time with my family.

meaning: More than one person.
example: There are many people in the park.

meaning: A place where children go to learn.
example: We walk to school every morning.

meaning: The early part of the day.

meaning: A boy or man who has the same parents as you.

meaning: A girl or woman who has the same parents as you.

meaning: Wanting or needing food.

meaning: Needing a drink of water.

meaning: The state of the air or atmosphere (rain, wind, sun).
example: The weather is beautiful and sunny today.

meaning: Not easy to do or understand.

meaning: Having a great value or meaning.
example: Water is important for our health.

meaning: A room or building with many books to read or borrow.

meaning: The part of the day between afternoon and night.
example: We watch television in the evening.

meaning: Not inside a building.

meaning: Liked more than other people or things.
example: Apple is my favorite fruit.

meaning: Something bad that happens by chance.
example: He had a small accident on his bicycle.

meaning: To have the same opinion as someone.
example: I agree with you about this idea.

meaning: To think that something is true.
example: I believe that you can pass the exam.

meaning: Paying attention to avoid danger or mistakes.

meaning: To make something new.

meaning: To make a choice after thinking.
example: I cannot decide which shoe to buy.

meaning: To make something clear or easy to understand.

meaning: Known by many people in many places.

meaning: To make something better than before.

meaning: An act of traveling from one place to another.

meaning: To keep someone or something safe from harm.

meaning: To get or accept something that is sent.
example: I receive an email from my father every week.

meaning: To need something or make it necessary.
example: Most plants require sunlight to grow.

meaning: To share an idea or plan for someone to think about.

meaning: Helping you to do or get what you want.
example: A dictionary is a very useful book.

meaning: Very surprising, wonderful, or excellent.
example: We saw an amazing sunset at the beach.

meaning: To take something from someone and return it later.

meaning: To say that you are not happy about something.
example: Customers complain when the service is slow.

meaning: To find or learn something for the first time.
example: Scientists discover new plants in the forest.

meaning: To give someone confidence and hope.
example: Teachers encourage students to read more.

meaning: Strong and not sick.
example: Eating fresh fruit keeps you healthy.

meaning: To form a picture or idea of something in your mind.
example: Can you imagine living on a warm island?

meaning: To design or make something for the first time.
example: They want to invent a flying car.

meaning: Something that is done wrong.
example: I made a small mistake on my test.

meaning: A person who lives near you.
example: Our neighbor is very friendly and kind.

meaning: What you think or believe about something.
example: What is your opinion on this new movie?

meaning: To bring back information into your mind.

meaning: Achieving the results that you wanted.
example: He is a very successful businessman.

meaning: To go from one place to another.
example: I want to travel to Spain next summer.

meaning: To make something ready for use.
example: We need to prepare for the test tomorrow.

meaning: To leave someone or something forever.
example: The sailors had to abandon the sinking ship.

meaning: Correct in all details; exact.
example: We need an accurate map of the forest.

meaning: To successfully finish or get something by working hard.
example: She worked hard to achieve her dream.

meaning: To get or obtain something.
example: He managed to acquire a rare stamp for his collection.

meaning: To change your behavior or ideas to fit a new situation.
example: It is easy for children to adapt to a new school.

meaning: To study something carefully to understand it.

meaning: Lasting for only a short time; using few words.

meaning: A new or difficult task that tests your ability.

meaning: Physical harm that breaks or spoils something.

meaning: Extremely important and necessary.
example: Water is essential for all living things.

meaning: A promise that something will definitely happen or be true.

meaning: Happening often.
example: He is a frequent visitor to our local library.

meaning: To say what will happen in the future.

meaning: To find an answer or solution to a problem.
example: They need to resolve their argument quickly.

meaning: As much as is needed; enough.
example: Do we have sufficient food for the guests?

meaning: To come near or nearer to something in space or time.

meaning: To think that something is true, without proof.

meaning: An advantage or useful effect that you get from something.

meaning: A result of a particular action or situation.
example: The consequence of being late was missing the train.

meaning: To make someone believe that something is true.
example: I managed to convince him to join our team.

meaning: Extremely important or serious; or saying what is wrong.
example: The doctor said his condition is critical but stable.

meaning: To show that something is very important.
example: Teachers emphasize the importance of practice.

meaning: To guess the size, cost, or value of something roughly.
example: They estimate the project will take three weeks.

meaning: To study or examine something carefully to find the truth.
example: Police will investigate the cause of the fire.

meaning: To make something continue in the same way or condition.

meaning: To get something that you want, especially through effort.
example: You must obtain permission before entering.

meaning: To disagree with or try to stop a plan or idea.
example: Many locals oppose building a new highway here.

meaning: To refuse to accept or believe something.
example: The school had to reject his application.

meaning: Large or important enough to have an effect or be noticed.
example: There was a significant improvement in her grades.

meaning: To change the shape, appearance, or character of something completely.
example: Computers can transform the way we learn.

meaning: Including all, or almost all, the items or facts that may be involved.
example: The report gives a comprehensive overview of the issue.

meaning: Extremely important because it will affect other things.

meaning: An event that causes a lot of damage, destruction, or pain.

meaning: Working well and quickly without wasting time, money, or energy.
example: An efficient heater uses less electricity.

meaning: Able to change to fit new situations, or easy to bend.

meaning: To produce or create something.

meaning: An idea or explanation that is based on a few facts but is not yet proven.
example: Scientists are testing their new hypothesis in the lab.

meaning: To suggest that something is true without saying it directly.
example: His silence seemed to imply that he agreed with us.

meaning: To combine two or more things so that they work together.
example: We want to integrate the new software with our system.

meaning: To show or prove that a decision or action is reasonable or right.
example: You need to justify why you spent so much money.

meaning: To increase something as much as possible.
example: We must maximize our efforts to finish on time.

meaning: To find an acceptable way of dealing with two opposing ideas or situations.

meaning: On the subject of; connected with.
example: I told him about my new school.

meaning: Something you say or write when someone asks a question.
example: She wrote the correct answer on the board.

meaning: A living thing that is not a human or a plant.
example: A dog is a very friendly animal.

meaning: An area of sand or small stones next to the sea.
example: We like to swim and play at the beach.

meaning: At or towards the back of something.
example: The cat is hiding behind the big chair.

meaning: In or into the space separating two things.

meaning: A glass or plastic container for keeping liquids.
example: He filled the bottle with cold water.

meaning: A device for taking photographs or making films.
example: She took a beautiful picture with her new camera.

meaning: A round stick of wax that gives light when it burns.
example: They lit a candle when the electricity went out.

meaning: Not dirty; free from dirt.
example: You should always keep your room clean.

meaning: A white or gray shape in the sky made of tiny drops of water.
example: A big dark cloud covered the sun.

meaning: A person whose job is to make sick people well.

meaning: Near the beginning of a period of time; before the usual time.

meaning: To get pleasure from something.
example: We enjoy playing football in the afternoon.

meaning: A large area of land covered with trees.

meaning: An area of land next to a house where plants and flowers grow.

meaning: A hard, clear substance used to make windows and bottles.

meaning: A building that people live in.

meaning: A piece of land completely surrounded by water.

meaning: The room where you cook and prepare food.

meaning: A meal that you eat in the middle of the day.

meaning: A public place where people buy and sell food and goods.

meaning: A unit of time equal to sixty seconds.

meaning: Not at any time; not on any occasion.

meaning: A room or building where people work at desks.

meaning: Thin material made from wood, used for writing or drawing.

meaning: A natural flow of water that goes across land into the sea.

meaning: A small piece of paper that shows you have paid for a journey or event.

meaning: A very small town in the countryside.

meaning: An opening in a wall or vehicle that lets in light and air.

meaning: A thing that you do for interest or pleasure.

meaning: An opinion or suggestion about what someone should do.
example: My teacher gave me good advice on how to study.

meaning: A place where airplanes land and take off.

meaning: A piece of writing about a particular subject in a newspaper or website.

meaning: A small round object that you press to operate a machine, or use to fasten clothes.
example: Press this blue button to start the machine.

meaning: The city where the government of a country is.

meaning: A period of one hundred years.

meaning: Making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to use.

meaning: A person who buys goods or services from a shop or business.

meaning: To become smaller or less, or to make something smaller or less.

meaning: A unit for measuring temperature; or an academic title.
example: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.

meaning: The amount of space between two places.

meaning: The physical or mental strength needed to do something.

meaning: Physical activity that you do to stay healthy and strong.

meaning: From or connected with a country that is not your own.

meaning: The time that will come after the present.

meaning: The study of past events.

meaning: To ask someone to come to a social event.
example: She wants to invite all her classmates to her party.

meaning: A short piece of information that you send to someone.

meaning: A building where historical or scientific objects are kept.

meaning: The physical world, including plants, animals, and landscapes.

meaning: Liked or enjoyed by a large number of people.

meaning: To tell someone that you will definitely do something.

meaning: The cause of an event or situation; why something happens.

meaning: The study of the physical world based on facts and experiments.

meaning: The set of conditions and facts that affect a person or place.

meaning: An area of knowledge that you study in school.

meaning: An event or thing that you did not expect.

meaning: A measure of how hot or cold something is.

meaning: To present a product or service publicly to persuade people to buy it.

meaning: A quality or feature that is a natural part of someone or something.

meaning: A group of people or things with similar features.

meaning: A fact or condition connected with or relevant to an event or action.

meaning: To promise or pledge to do something; or to perform a crime.

meaning: To show clearly that something is true or how it works.

meaning: The natural world in which people, animals, and plants live.

meaning: To spend money, time, or effort on something to get a benefit later.

meaning: The largest amount, size, or number that is possible or allowed.

meaning: The smallest amount, size, or number that is possible or allowed.

meaning: To record your name or information on an official list.

meaning: A plan or system for doing or organizing something.

meaning: The social, professional, or legal position of someone or something.

meaning: Connected with practical science, machines, or special skills.

meaning: Lasting for only a short or limited time.

meaning: The main subject of a talk, book, or exhibition.

meaning: A set of formal ideas that are intended to explain something.

meaning: A special set of clothes worn by all members of an organization.

meaning: The amount of space that something fills; or the level of sound.

meaning: Easy to see or understand; clear.

meaning: The main or largest part of something; or large size.

meaning: To fall down suddenly because of being weak or broken.

meaning: One of the parts that make up a machine, system, or idea.

meaning: The opposite of what has been said or expected.

meaning: To organize different parts of an activity so they work together.

meaning: To appear or come out from somewhere.

meaning: A basic structure or set of ideas that supports something.

meaning: An amount of money saved or collected for a special purpose.

meaning: Happening at the very beginning; first.

meaning: Connected with the inside of something (a body, machine, or country).

meaning: Easy to see, notice, or understand.
example: The answer to this question is very obvious.

meaning: To fill or use a space, area, or amount of time.
example: Reading can occupy your free time productively.

meaning: The final result or effect of an action or situation.

meaning: The study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence.

meaning: A part or share of a whole.

meaning: To fight against authority or a government.

meaning: To control an activity or process by using rules.

meaning: Safe from danger, harm, or loss; or to make safe.

meaning: To move or change from one position or direction to another.

meaning: To state or name something clearly and exactly.

meaning: A piece of work that must be done, especially one that is difficult.

meaning: To move someone or something from one place to another.

meaning: The health, happiness, and safety of a person or group.

meaning: The act of getting or acquiring something; or something acquired.
example: The acquisition of language happens quickly in childhood.

meaning: Connected with beauty and art, or the study of beautiful things.
example: The building has a modern and pleasing aesthetic.

meaning: The state of having more than one possible meaning; or lack of clarity.
example: We need to clear up any ambiguity in the contract.

meaning: Similar in some way to another thing, making comparison possible.

meaning: Based on random choice or personal whim, rather than any reason or system.

meaning: A confident and forceful statement of fact or belief.

meaning: Forming a united and well-organized whole.

meaning: Able to exist, live, or work together without problems.

meaning: Formed an idea or plan in your mind.

meaning: An opinion or decision that all members of a group agree on.
example: The committee reached a consensus on the new rules.

meaning: The quality of being trusted and believed in.
example: The scientist's research has high credibility.

meaning: A change or difference from what is usual, expected, or standard.

meaning: Treating a person or group unfairly because of their race, sex, or religion.
example: The law protects workers against discrimination.

meaning: The act of twisting or changing facts so they are no longer true.

meaning: To experience or meet something unexpectedly, especially a problem.

meaning: Connected with beliefs about what is right and wrong.
example: It is not ethical to copy someone else's work.

meaning: Clear and easy to understand, leaving no doubt about the meaning.
example: She gave explicit instructions on how to finish.

meaning: A possible effect or result of an action or decision.

meaning: Existing as a natural and permanent quality of something.
example: There is an inherent risk in climbing high mountains.

meaning: Belonging naturally; essential and basic.

meaning: Small, minor, or not very important.
example: There was only a marginal improvement in the weather.

meaning: A typical example or pattern of something; a model.
example: This new technology is a paradigm shift for business.

meaning: A fact or event that can be observed, especially something unusual.
example: A rainbow is a beautiful natural phenomenon.

meaning: Showing great knowledge, understanding, or deep emotion.
example: His words had a profound effect on my thoughts.

meaning: Connected with how good something is, rather than how much.
example: They did a qualitative study of user behavior.

meaning: Happening or coming after something else.
example: The first book was great, and subsequent ones were even better.

meaning: The state of being logically or legally correct and acceptable.
example: The judge questioned the validity of his statement.
